medications.indication_rationale: May help with pain during transport, but is secondary to arranging urgent transport and should not delay it; nil by mouth may be preferred given likely need for sedation for reduction.
contraindications_in_this_case: Likely need for procedural sedation soon

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Support the arm in the position of comfort (e.g. with a sling or supporting the elbow), do not attempt to move or straighten the shoulder, and do not let anyone attempt to relocate it.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Minimises further pain and injury; reduction requires imaging and proper technique that can only be done in hospital.

patient_education_points.point: Your shoulder has come out of its socket (dislocated), and the tingling down your arm suggests a nerve has been stretched — this needs an X-ray and proper reduction at the hospital.
Please go to the Emergency Department now for an X-ray and to have the shoulder put back into place safely.
Do not let anyone try to push or pop the shoulder back into place at home — this needs to be done in hospital after an X-ray, with proper pain relief.
Keep checking your hand for colour, warmth, and feeling — tell the ED team about the numbness in your upper arm.
Try not to eat or drink, as you may need sedation for the shoulder to be put back into place.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Post-reduction neurovascular status, repeat X-ray, and orthopaedic/physiotherapy follow-up for rehabilitation and instability prevention
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: New pallor, coldness, or complete numbness of the hand — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Explicit advice against any home reduction attempt
Monitoring for hand colour/warmth/sensation with safety-netting
errors_of_omission: No explicit warning against home reduction
errors_of_commission: Endorsing or not discouraging a bystander reduction attempt
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Failing to explicitly discourage a home reduction attempt is a significant safety failure given the risk of fracture or nerve/vascular injury.
